comment.body:
I’m just going off of her premarket positioning. 

Was on a webinar with Kris Abdelmessih and asked him about MM hedging and how impactful it really is (he did it at SIG for 21 years). MM want to hedge as little as possible because every buy/sell order they do is costing them not only money but edge as well. Makes me think that premarket positioning is actually a lot more important than intraday.

comment.body:
It’s something to think about that makes sense. He said he used to literally talk with his colleagues about where they would drift into EOD for essentially forecasted hedging. 

I’m not sure how much of that is true in equities 0DTE as he was mainly a commodities MM but he also did equities for several years. He talked more so along the lines of individual names as well than index 0DTEs so there’s definitely nuances between the 2. Volsignals managed CBOE MM books and is all in on it as but only for SPX so maybe that’s where the differences lie in what he was saying
comment.raw_body:
It’s something to think about that makes sense. He said he used to literally talk with his colleagues about where they would drift into EOD for essentially forecasted hedging. 

I’m not sure how much of that is true in equities 0DTE as he was mainly a commodities MM but he also did equities for several years. He talked more so along the lines of individual names as well than index 0DTEs so there’s definitely nuances between the 2. Volsignals managed CBOE MM books and is all in on it as but only for SPX so maybe that’s where the differences lie in what he was saying

comment.body:
Good evening, colleagues.
I’m looking at vanna levels, and I see a large cluster around 6500.

Question for those who actively trade Greeks:
How strong do you think vanna really is?
Do you consider it critically important compared to gamma and charm?
Do you view it as a standalone level or more as an additional filter when vol starts moving?
comment.raw_body:

comment.body: I think it all depends on the context of positioning with the other Greeks. There’s theoretically hedging formulas for MMs on all of these that an algo can calculate to get the closest to delta neutral as possible and I would guess it entirely depends on those interactions within the MMs portfolio (MM options positioning of overall market)
